Our clients provide equal opportunities to all its employees and all qualified applicants for employment, without regard to their race, caste, religion, color, ancestry, marital status, sex, age, nationality, disability, and veteran status. Employees of our client shall be treated with dignity and in accordance with their policy to maintain a work environment free of sexual harassment, whether physical, verbal, or psychological. Employee policies and practices shall be administered in a manner that would ensure that in all matters equal opportunity is provided to those eligible and the decisions are merit-based.

• Drive MLOps/LLMOps practices: CI/CD, prompt/model versioning, automated evaluations, drift/quality monitoring, cost controls, canary releases, and rollback strategies.

• Embed payments grade security, privacy, and compliance: IAM least privilege, encryption/KMS, secrets management, PII controls, threat modeling, and audit evidence.

• Collaborate with internal teams and technology partners to ensure smooth implementation, performance tuning, and production readiness across environments.

• Mentor teams and evangelize an AI engineering culture through reusable reference architectures, best practices, knowledge sharing, and technical governance.•


Roles & Responsibilities

An ask from an AI architect should drive the strategy, good in communication, client management and drive the business strategy

Company Description

AI Knots is a digital partner specializing in AI-powered solutions for content creation, digital marketing ads, and marketing automation. With expertise in web and mobile platform development, CMS, CRM, and automated lead generation, AI Knots helps businesses enhance their online presence using advanced AI tools. Our services span AI-driven digital marketing, ad optimization, SEO, eCommerce solutions, customer experience enhancement, and digital transformation strategies. By leveraging cutting-edge technologies, we empower businesses to unlock their growth potential.




Role Description

We are seeking an AI Engineering Manager and Solutions Architect - Gen AI to join our team full-time at our Trincomalee location. This role involves overseeing and managing AI engineering teams to deliver innovative AI-driven solutions. Responsibilities include developing and optimizing machine learning models, designing AI architectures, analyzing data for actionable insights, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to align AI projects with business goals. In addition, the role will require ensuring best practices in AI implementation and working directly with clients to understand and address their requirements using cutting-edge AI technologies.




Requirements




Work with clients to design, develop, and deploy new architectures to support machine learning & automation applications
Leverage advanced technical skills in modern data architecture, data science engineering, data transformation, and management of structured and unstructured data sources using cloud computing or on-prem technologies
Design and lead development on scalable, high-performance data architecture solutions that supports both the client business as well as AI/GenAI use cases
Build robust orchestration and automation workflows integrating multi-agent capabilities.
Develop and maintain pipelines for training, benchmarking, validating, and deploying LLMs and autonomous agents into production environments.
Conduct prompt engineering and fine-tuning of LLMs for domain-specific tasks.
Evaluate and iterate model performance based on metrics and user feedback
Define and lead technology proof of concepts to ensure feasibility of new data and cloud technology solutions
Display strong thought leadership and execution in pursuit of modern data architecture principles and technology modernization
Mentor, motivate, and coach junior members on technical best practices and inspire professional development
Own the continuous optimization of system prompts and instructions for LLMs (Gemini, OpenAI, Grok, Claude, Co-Pilot, DeepSeek, Perplexity) to ensure Velo's response is accurate, tone is consistent, and on-brand.







Qualifications

Proficiency in Data Science, including statistical analysis and data visualization techniques
Strong Analytical Skills and practical experience with data-driven decision-making
Expertise in Machine Learning, including model development and evaluation
Skills in Data Mining and large-scale data processing
Experience in designing AI architectures and solutions aligned with business objectives
Exceptional problem-solving abilities and ability to lead technical teams
Strong verbal and written communication skills
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or a related field
Familiarity with AI tools and frameworks
Experience in digital marketing with Social Media Automations is a plus
Experience in eCommerce platforms Automations is a plus

Title: Insights Analyst
Description:
Company Description

At Mission Critical Group (MCG), we don’t just manufacture electrical systems—we deliver power reliability where it matters most. From data centers to critical infrastructure, our solutions keep the world running. With more than one million square feet of U.S. manufacturing capacity, we move fast, build smart, and never compromise on quality.

Job Description

The Insights Analyst supports enterprise-level visibility, data governance, and operational analytics across the Electrical Equipment Center of Excellence (EECOE).

This role is not limited to Customer Success metrics. It provides structured reporting and insight across Safety, Quality, Project Management, Engineering, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, Business Operations, and EECOE KPIs.

The Primary Focus Is Ensuring Data Is


Clearly defined
Consistently captured
Accurately represented
Structured for enterprise reporting
Transitioned effectively from legacy tools into scalable platforms


This is a builder role - not just a reporting role.

Key Responsibilities

Enterprise Reporting & Visibility


Develop standardized reporting cadence across EECOE functions
Build executive-ready dashboards and reporting views
Translate operational data into actionable insights
Identify performance trends, risk signals, and gaps


Data Governance & KPI Standardization


Define KPI structures and reporting definitions
Align cross-functional teams on common data language
Eliminate duplicate tracking systems
Improve data accuracy and trustworthiness


System & Data Transformation


Support transition from legacy tracking tools (Excel, Airtable, standalone trackers) into enterprise platforms (Wrike, ERP, Vault, etc.)
Ensure data integrity during migrations
Partner with Business Process Owners to align workflows to reporting needs


Executive & Strategic Enablement


Support enterprise initiatives with analytics
Create forward-looking insights (not just historical reporting)
Provide transparency into execution, risk, and progress
Support cross-functional performance alignment


What Success Looks Like (6–12 Months)


Unified reporting structure across EECOE
Clear KPI definitions documented and adopted
Reduced reliance on siloed spreadsheets
Improved executive visibility into operational performance
Stronger data integrity during system transitions
Leaders rely on this role for clarity and decision support


Qualifications


3–7+ years in analytics, business operations, enterprise reporting, or operational strategy
Advanced Excel proficiency (required)
Experience with BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, etc.)
Experience supporting system implementations or data transitions
Strong understanding of data governance principles
Experience in industrial, manufacturing, or operational environments preferred


Additional Information

A Note to our Recruitment Partners: We really appreciate the interest, but MCG currently manages hiring through our internal team. We love getting to know our candidates directly! Because of this, we don’t accept unsolicited resumes from agencies at this time. If we ever need an extra hand, we’ll be sure to reach out to the community. Thanks for understanding!

DVM is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to qualified individuals with disabilities and to act in accordance with regulations and guidance issued by the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC).
